macro: The Fed has held its policy rate at 3.50%-3.75% for four straight meetings, and new Chair Kevin Warsh used back-to-back House and Senate testimony this week to frame policy as strictly data-dependent, invoking "price stability" repeatedly rather than offering forward guidance. June CPI cooled sharply to 3.5% annually (a 0.4% monthly drop versus a forecast -0.2%), and June PPI followed a day later with its first monthly decline since August 2025, down 0.3% as gasoline fell 12%. Prediction markets now put the odds of a July 28-29 hold near 90%, even as Iran-linked shipping disruption keeps an inflation tail risk alive in energy. The date to watch this week is Friday, when June housing starts and the preliminary July University of Michigan sentiment reading land back to back, testing whether June's rebound from record-low confidence is holding.
btc_status: Bitcoin trades near $64,800, consolidating just above its roughly $62,500 200-week moving average after briefly closing beneath it in late June for the first time in four years. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index reads 25 (Extreme Fear); the level to watch is a reclaim of $65,600 resistance (the 50-day EMA), with a break below $62,000 support reopening a path toward this cycle's $58,300 low.
markets:
TOP OPPORTUNITIES
1. WFC — 71/100 | Q2 EPS of $2.00 beat the $1.72 consensus by 16% on a broad bank-earnings beat, with shares trading above both their 50-day and 200-day averages.
2. ETH — 61/100 | Spot ETH ETFs pulled in $58.3M as price presses toward the $2,000 resistance level, though RSI near 71 signals stretched near-term momentum.

AVOID TODAY
SNDK — Down roughly 6% today as profit-taking hits the priciest AI-memory winners after SK Hynix's Q2 estimate cut; still up about 640% year-to-date and vulnerable to further unwind.
energy: WTI trades near $80.17 a barrel, up about 1% on the session, while Brent sits near $84.63, both still elevated after the US resumed strikes on Iran and reinstated a naval presence near the Strait of Hormuz. The EIA's latest weekly report (week ended July 10, released July 15) showed crude inventories fell 1.69 million barrels, a smaller draw than the 2.1 million barrel forecast, with gasoline stocks running 8.4% below their five-year seasonal average.
vix_and_dollar: The VIX closed at 15.67 on July 15, down about 5% on the session and sitting at the low end of the 15-20 "normal" band even with Middle East tensions unresolved. The dollar index (DXY) eased to 100.79, down 0.13%, a combination suggesting risk appetite is holding up despite the oil-driven inflation risk still on the table.
mortgage_rate: The 30-year fixed mortgage averaged 6.49% in Freddie Mac's survey for the week of July 9, 2026, up from 6.43% the week before. Rates near six-month highs are colliding with record home prices, keeping the existing-home sales pace near multi-year lows even as affordability technically improves on wage growth.
personal_finance_deep_dive: Care.com's 2026 Cost of Care Report found the average parent now spends 20% of household income on child care, nearly triple the 7% threshold the Department of Health and Human Services considers affordable. Single parents are hit hardest, with child care eating an estimated 35% of median household income versus about 10% for married couples, and 31% of all parents say they're pulling from savings to cover the gap. The strain compounds an already fragile household balance sheet: with the personal savings rate near multi-year lows and credit card debt above $1.2 trillion, care costs are becoming a structural drag on any household's ability to build a cushion at all.
wealth_building_deep_dive: Devenir's 2025 year-end research report put total HSA assets at $174 billion across 41.7 million accounts, continuing a multi-year climb as more employers pair the accounts with high-deductible health plans. The appeal is the triple tax break: contributions go in pre-tax, investment growth inside the account is never taxed, and withdrawals for qualified medical expenses are tax-free too, a structure no 401(k) or IRA matches. For 2026 the contribution limit rises to $4,400 for individuals and $8,750 for families, with a $1,000 catch-up for savers 55 and older; a reader who maxes the account and invests the balance rather than spending it as a checking account effectively builds a stealth retirement fund, since after age 65 non-medical withdrawals are taxed like a traditional IRA instead of penalized.
notable: SK Hynix posted its worst single-session decline on record in Seoul (-15.4%, July 13) after a broker cut its Q2 profit estimate, dragging Micron, SanDisk, and Western Digital down 3%-6% this week.

Spot Bitcoin and Ether ETFs both logged net inflows this week ($191M and $58M respectively) after a rough June, though the bitcoin figure reversed a $424.7M single-day outflow on July 13 — flows remain choppy.

Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Goldman Sachs all beat Q2 estimates this week on the back of near-record trading revenue, kicking off bank earnings season with broad upside surprises.
